puzzles.title: Return Hello
puzzles.prompt: Complete the function so it returns the string "Hello".
puzzles.narrative: Rust says hello. It seems friendly. It is not friendly.
hints: String::from() creates an owned String
functions return the last expression without a semicolon
String::from("Hello")

puzzles.title: Sum a Vector
puzzles.prompt: Return the sum of all numbers in the vector.
puzzles.narrative: A vector. Not an array. The distinction will matter later.
hints: iterators have a sum() method
v.iter() creates an iterator over references
v.iter().sum()

puzzles.title: Reverse a String
puzzles.prompt: Reverse the input string without using built-in reverse().
puzzles.narrative: Strings in Rust are not simple. Nothing in Rust is simple.
hints: chars() iterates over characters
rev() reverses an iterator
s.chars().rev().collect()

puzzles.title: Count Characters
puzzles.prompt: Count how many times the character 'x' appears in the string.
puzzles.narrative: Count the x's. There are always more x's than you expect.
hints: chars() gives you an iterator of characters
filter() keeps only matching elements
s.chars().filter(|&c| c == 'x').count()

puzzles.title: Filter Even Numbers
puzzles.prompt: Return a new vector containing only even numbers.
puzzles.narrative: Only the even ones. The odd ones belong to the compiler now.
hints: iter() creates an iterator, into_iter() consumes the vector
filter() takes a closure that returns bool
v.into_iter().filter(|x| x % 2 == 0).collect()

puzzles.title: Ownership Fix
puzzles.prompt: Fix the function so it returns the length without causing a move error.
puzzles.hint: text must still be valid after calling length()
puzzles.narrative: You tried to use something you already gave away. The compiler remembers.
hints: the function already takes a reference — &String
references do not move ownership
s.len() returns the length of a String

puzzles.title: Borrow Checker Challenge
puzzles.prompt: Fix the code so it compiles — mutable and immutable borrows must not overlap.
puzzles.hint: r1 and r2 cannot exist at the same time
puzzles.narrative: Two hands reaching for the same thing. The compiler cut one off.
hints: immutable and mutable borrows cannot coexist
r1's lifetime must end before r2 is created
use r1 before creating r2 — scope determines lifetime

puzzles.title: Implement Struct Method
puzzles.prompt: Finish the Point struct so distance() returns the Manhattan distance.
puzzles.narrative: A struct. An impl. A self. The language is building a mirror of the world.
hints: self refers to the current instance
i32 has an abs() method for absolute value
self.x.abs() + self.y.abs()

puzzles.title: Lifetimes Nightmare
puzzles.prompt: Fix the function so it returns a reference safely using lifetimes.
puzzles.narrative: The compiler wants to know how long things live. Everything has an expiry now.
hints: the lifetime 'a is already declared — both inputs share it
the return reference lives as long as the shorter input
if a.len() >= b.len() { a } else { b }
